text: Now this is the portion of, this is the place where we, in this case we are going to start dissecting the bladder because bladder is too advanced. Now push it inside. Yes. Cable work. Cable thoda work. If you go here, you are going to open the bladder. So, in this case, know the extent of the bladder and then dissect.

The surgeon identifies the plane for dissection near the bladder, applying steady traction to carefully separate the tissue. The dissection progresses to the under surface of the bladder, with the peritoneum being lifted and cut to expose the bladder wall.

The surgeon retroverts the anatomical structures, revealing the bladder and fibers in front of the surgical field. The cervix is identified, and the surgeon proceeds to cut the tissue at the lateral boundary.

So it doesn't make any difference. If you stick to the basics, you can dissect it. Only thing is that lateral window. Lateral window is what? You go from the normal area to the abnormal adhesions. Don't straight away go and try to cut. That was the reason I was trying to go in the normal area and try to cut it. But there was no normal area left.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the bladder, having carefully separated it from surrounding tissues.
